payload.answer: A single 8000-word transcript cannot be durably committed as a single memory card due to: (1) lack of explicit segmentation into discrete statements, (2) unresolved speaker/role ambiguity, (3) potential superseded/deleted content, and (4) violation of the 'preserve ambiguity' constraint when no contextual boundaries exist. Durable memory requires atomic, timestamped, and role-attributed statements.
citations: Schema requirement: 'preserve ambiguity' → Ambiguity in speaker roles/intent cannot be resolved without granular parsing.
Schema requirement: 'never present superseded/deleted facts' → No mechanism to distinguish active vs. inactive content in monolithic transcript.
Schema requirement: 'durable memory' → 8000 words exceed atomicity thresholds for a single card (max ~250 words for context-preserving durability).

entity_resolution.reason: No discrete, actionable entities or relationships can be extracted from an 8000-word transcript without granular, context-preserving segmentation. Ambiguity in intent, speaker roles, and temporal anchoring violates durability requirements.

details: Input violates durability constraints: monolithic transcript lacks atomicity, temporal anchoring, and role attribution.
Ambiguity preservation impossible without segmentation; current form risks false precision.
No superseded/deleted content detection mechanism exists for unparsed transcripts.

repair_options: Segment transcript into timestamped, role-attributed statements (≤250 words/card) with explicit speaker/intent resolution.
Flag as 'raw_transcript' with metadata for future granular processing, but do not commit as memory.
Request clarification on critical entities/relationships before attempting durability encoding.
